manifest_note: Figures authored directly into papers/I1/figures/ via Phase 7a sub-wave 7a.2 figure-authoring; no review_figures.py manifest emitted yet (these are bundle-bespoke schematic and worked-case figures, not visualizations.py outputs).
anchors_consulted: The supersession ledger pattern + Pipeline Invariant #13 (Wave 6)

worked_case_figures_with_aristotle_run_i.cited_in_caption: single Aristotle priority batch (no specific run ID)
worked_case_figures_with_aristotle_run_i.advisory: Caption states 'single Aristotle priority batch' but does not pin a specific run ID. Per Tier-3 Stage-13 profile this is acceptable when the batch is reproducible from ARISTOTLE_THEOREMS registry; promote to specific run ID at Stage 10 if registry resolves it.

figures.name: i1_fig1_three_layer_architecture

physics.notes: Layer 1 metadata strip lists canonical-module pointers (formulas.py, constants.py, visualizations.py, tests/, wkb/, adw/, vestigial/) consistent with CLAUDE.md Pipeline Invariants #1-3. Layer 2 strip cites '5229 theorems / 1 axiom / zero sorry' which is consistent with the Phase 7a.0.2 counts.json regen baseline (memory: 5229 theorems / 5204 substantive / 0 sorry / 1 axiom). Layer 3 strip cites Aristotle run 270e77a0 which matches the FirstOrderKMS worked case in §3.

style.notes: Three-block stack with steel-blue / amber / sage palette is colorblind-accessible per CLAUDE.md (avoids red/green pairing). The single carmine accent is the curved feedback arrow on the right which legitimately marks the counterexample/refinement direction (semantic use, not a data series — acceptable). Font is consistent serif. Title and subtitle hierarchy clear.

figures.notes: The asymmetric-composition statement in the subtitle ('none alone suffices') matches the §2 paper thesis. Curved 'counterexample or refinement' label on the right edge is faint but legible at full resolution. No clipping or rendering artifacts.

figures.name: i1_fig2_pipeline_14_stages

issues: Minor: gate-text in several cells appears truncated at the right edge — 'Constants & param[s]' (S1), 'Lean-theorem ref pre[…]' (S2), 'claims-reviewer pas[s]' (S10), '[ga]te: fig functions registered' (S8 left-edge clipping). The text strings are still readable in context but the truncation is visible at the rendered resolution. Cause: cell width tight relative to gate-text length.

physics.notes: All 15 cells present (S1, S2, S3a, S3b, S4–S14). New stages (3a, 13, 14) correctly highlighted in amber per the legend; existing stages in steel blue. Stage labels match WAVE_EXECUTION_PIPELINE.md and the paper §6 prose. Stage 9 (Figure review) gate 'physics-qa pass' correctly self-references this very review.

style.notes: 5×3 grid layout. Steel-blue / amber palette colorblind-safe per CLAUDE.md. Legend in lower-left clearly distinguishes new vs existing stages.

figures.notes: The figure delivers the round-1 recommendation: 14-stage pipeline summary with new-vs-existing stage distinction. The truncated gate-text is a minor advisory; readers can always cross-reference Table 1 (\input tables/table1_stages.tex on line 569) which is autogenerated from WAVE_EXECUTION_PIPELINE.md. The caption explicitly cites the table, so the figure plus table together carry the load.

figures.name: i1_fig3_sentence_state_clusters

rendering.notes: Some node labels (B2, B5, A4, A6, C5, C6) appear in lighter grey because they are 'unclustered sentence' nodes per the legend; this is intentional encoding, not a rendering issue.

physics.notes: Three-paper layout (Paper A Tier 1, Paper B Tier 1, Paper C Tier 2) with six sentences each. Cluster C2 (amber, cross_bundle: true) correctly spans all three papers per the §8 caption claim. Single-bundle clusters C1 (steel blue, paper A internal) and C3 (sage, paper B internal) are distinct. Edge style (dashed for single-bundle, solid for cross-bundle) helps disambiguate the cross_bundle: true semantic.

style.notes: Steel-blue / amber / sage palette consistent with project convention. Greying-out of unclustered nodes is a reasonable choice that keeps the focal clusters salient.

figures.notes: Schematic figure (caption explicitly says 'schematic') so no specific run ID required. Figure successfully visualizes the cross_bundle: true cluster pattern and the validate.py CHECK 21 (bundle_consistency) target.

figures.name: i1_fig4_firstorderkms_grid

physics.notes: Left panel (original weak axiom): 4 of 9 cells colored steel-blue ('constrained'): r1, r2, i1 plus i2 highlighted carmine ('counterexample i2 = 1'). 5 cells in grey ('free'): r3, r4, r5, r6, i3. This matches the §3 claim that the original axiom 'constrained four of the nine component-level identities' with the counterexample vector c = (0,0,0,0,0,0,0,1,0). Right panel (corrected FirstOrderKMS): all 9 cells amber ('constrained'), with subtitle 'all 9 constrained, i3 = 0 explicit' matching the §3 prose ('strengthened axiom FirstOrderKMS encodes all seven; … i3 = 0; … r3 = r4 = r5 = r6 = 0'). Aristotle run ID 270e77a0 cited in the title — Tier-3 reproducibility requirement met.

style.notes: Steel-blue / amber / carmine palette colorblind-accessible. 3×3 grid layout matches the round-1 style recommendation ('Could be a 3×3 grid of KMS-relation cells; binary coloring').

figures.notes: The figure precisely visualizes the §3 narrative. Audit note: the figure correctly shows 4 originally-constrained components (r1, r2, i1, i2) with i2 highlighted as the counterexample, while the §3 prose states 'four of the nine component-level identities' and 'one nonzero noise channel' — the i2 = 1 highlight matches both. The right-panel subtitle's 'all 9 constrained' matches the §3 claim that the strengthened axiom 'closes cleanly'.

figures.name: i1_fig5_gap_counterexample

physics.notes: Function plotted: Δ(G) on [0, 12] with the parameterization N_f = 1, Λ = 1, c_4 = 1 stated in the title. G_c (small G) marks the bifurcation from the trivial branch Δ = 0 (dotted grey) to the non-trivial branch (steel-blue solid). G* ≈ 6.518 = 2/(1 - log 2) marks the Δ = Λ saturation point (amber circle). Amber-shaded region G ≥ G* is where the folklore claim Δ < Λ fails. The annotation 'folklore claim Δ < Λ fails for G ≥ G*' with the 'Aristotle run 79e07d55' subtitle directly addresses the §4 claim about the corrected statement. The numerical value G* = 2/(1 - log 2) ≈ 6.518 matches the saturation point shown on the plot and is consistent with the §4 prose.

style.notes: Standard publication-style plot with serif typography. Steel-blue main curve, amber marker for the saturation point, dotted grey for the trivial branch — colorblind-safe. Axis labels 'Coupling G' and 'Gap Δ' clearly readable. Legend in upper-left, no overlap with data. The amber-shaded G ≥ G* region is subtle but visible on careful inspection.

figures.notes: The only literal physics plot in the bundle (per round-1's note that this 'carries disproportionate weight in establishing the paper's empirical character'). Aristotle run ID 79e07d55 cited in caption — Tier-3 reproducibility requirement met.

figures.name: i1_fig6_chirality_wall_tree

physics.notes: Root node (carmine, top): 'sm_no_nu_R_ewbg_doubly_forbidden' — matches the theorem name in §5 prose. Two amber obstruction nodes: 'Z₁₆ chirality wall' (left/center) and 'Crossover (sphaleron suppression)' (right) — matches §5's 'two independent obstructions'. Three sage-green pillar nodes: 'Pillar A — anomaly', 'Pillar B — fermion content', 'Pillar C — wall form' — matches §5's three structural pillars (Z₁₆ anomaly chain / fermion-content / topological-pivoting-frame). Six steel-blue sublemma leaves visible (Sublemma A.1, B.1, C.1, 2.1, 2.2, 2.3) with two of the pillar boxes appearing to contain only one labeled sublemma each. The §5 prose claim is 'three sub-lemmas per pillar, plus three sub-lemmas for the crossover side, totaling nine'. The figure caption explicitly says 'sub-lemma names schematic' which acknowledges the simplified naming.

issues: Round-1 recommendation specified 'Top-down tree; root labeled with the original opaque axiom; leaves labeled with the 4 focused hypotheses + 1 dimensional assumption'. The implementation chose a treemap (nested-rectangles) layout instead of a strict top-down tree. Treemap is a reasonable layout choice for hierarchical data and the caption explicitly acknowledges the deviation ('treemap layout'), so this is an advisory not a fail.

Visible sublemma count: 6 leaf rectangles labeled (A.1, B.1, C.1, 2.1, 2.2, 2.3). The §5 narrative and the caption both reference 'nine sub-lemmas'. The visual count of labeled leaves is 6, not 9. Cause: the treemap layout consolidates same-pillar sublemmas into a single area where Pillar C visually contains a large unlabeled block that may represent the additional sublemmas C.2/C.3 implicit but not labeled. Either (a) annotate the unlabeled area with C.2/C.3 (or the relevant per-pillar sublemma names) or (b) revise the caption to read '9 sub-lemmas across 6 leaf groups' to match what the viewer actually sees. The substantive Tier-3 reproducibility claim ('all 9 sub-lemmas closed in a single Aristotle priority batch') is intact in the prose at §5 line 498-500; the figure-caption pairing is the gap.

No specific Aristotle run ID cited (caption refers only to 'a single Aristotle priority batch'). Tier-3 profile permits batch-level citation when a batch is registry-resolvable; flag for Stage-10 claims-reviewer to pin a specific batch identifier from ARISTOTLE_THEOREMS if available.

figures.notes: Substantive content correct; visualization choice (treemap vs strict tree) and labeled-leaf-count mismatch (6 visible vs 9 stated) is a Stage-9 → Stage-10 handoff item rather than a P0/P1 figure-rendering blocker. The figure successfully conveys 'monolithic root → multiple obstruction layers → tractable sublemmas' which is the substantive §5 claim, and reading the prose plus figure together is unambiguous. Recommendation: (i) add 3 missing sublemma labels to the treemap OR (ii) revise caption to acknowledge the 6-vs-9 visible-vs-actual count, plus pin Aristotle batch ID at Stage 10.

summary: Round-2 review of all 6 I1 bundle figures (i1_fig1 through i1_fig6) authored in response to round-1 recommendations I1-FIG-1 through I1-FIG-6. All 6 figures address their corresponding round-1 recommendation. Four pass cleanly (fig1, fig3, fig4, fig5). Two carry minor advisories (fig2: peripheral gate-text truncation in pipeline cells — readable in context, table 1 carries detail; fig6: treemap layout deviates from round-1's recommended top-down tree, 6 labeled leaves visible vs 9 sublemmas stated in prose, no Aristotle batch ID pinned in caption). Both advisories are Stage-9 → Stage-10 handoff items, not blockers. Tier-3 reproducibility audit: fig4 cites Aristotle run 270e77a0, fig5 cites run 79e07d55 — both worked-case data figures meet the Tier-3 'reproducible Aristotle run ID' anchor requirement. fig6 cites only a 'priority batch' label without specific run ID; advisory passed to Stage 10. Schematic figures (fig1, fig2, fig3) do not require run IDs per Tier-3 profile (which scopes the requirement to worked-case figures, not architecture/process diagrams). Overall verdict: PASS with minor advisories. Stage 9 status: green-with-advisories (yellow-leaning-green); recommend stage9_status='yellow' until fig6 caption/labeling resolved at Stage 10.

recommendations.title: Pipeline figure peripheral gate-text truncation
recommendations.action: Either widen each cell by ~12% or shorten gate-text strings (e.g., 'Constants & params' → 'Constants', 'claims-reviewer pass' → 'claims pass'). Defer-acceptable: caption + Table 1 already carry full gate-text, so figure-only truncation is non-blocking.

recommendations.title: Chirality-wall treemap labeled-leaf count and Aristotle batch ID
recommendations.action: Either (i) annotate the 3 currently-unlabeled treemap regions with their sublemma names (C.2, C.3, plus one in Pillar B) OR (ii) revise caption to clarify '9 sub-lemmas across the 6 labeled leaf groups'. Coordinate with Stage-10 claims-reviewer to pin a specific Aristotle batch ID from ARISTOTLE_THEOREMS if registry-resolvable; otherwise leave caption batch-level.

advisories.description: All 6 round-1 figure recommendations addressed; 0 P0/P1 blockers remain. Two P2-level advisories (fig2 truncation, fig6 leaf-count and batch ID) are stage-9 → stage-10 handoff items.

Per Stage-13 Tier-3 profile ('each worked case must trace to a reproducible Aristotle run ID OR commit-pinned counterexample'): fig4 (run 270e77a0) PASS; fig5 (run 79e07d55) PASS; fig6 cites batch but not specific run ID — Stage-10 to verify and pin if available.

Schematic figures (fig1, fig2, fig3) appropriately do not cite Aristotle run IDs (their content is architecture/process, not worked-case proof artifacts). Layer 3 strip in fig1 cites 270e77a0 as an illustrative example which is a stylistic choice consistent with the §3 cross-reference.
